{"cmd":["bash","-lc","python - <<'PY'\ncontent = \"\"\"## CHARACTER DOSSIERS: N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:** Psychological profiles, voice patterns, humor styles, and relationship dynamics for all characters appearing in N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"\n\n---\n\n## JHACE TORRINS\n\n**Age:** 29 Turns  \n**Profession:** Structural engineer, heat-grid inspector, forgemaster (unaffiliated with guild leadership)  \n**Physical:** Wiry build, weathered hands, permanent shoulder tension. Dark hair in short tail. Canvas work clothes, heavy leather tool belt. Moves efficiently, no wasted motion.\n\n### Core Psychology\n\n**Primary Driver: Obsessive Prevention Through Vigilance**\n- Parents died in building collapse when he was 7 Turns (23 people killed total)\n- Investigation concluded \"material fatigue\" – no one caught it in time\n- Has spent 22 Turns trying to be vigilant enough\n- Sees thousands of ways buildings can kill people\n- Cannot unsee potential failures\n\n**The Knot:**\n\"There are thousands of people in this city. I will spend the rest of my life unable to save most of them. I will feel responsible for all of them. This is what it means to have opened my eyes to what I can do.\"\n\n**Isolation as Armor:**\n- Works alone because teaching means inflicting his hypervigilance on others\n- Refuses apprentices (\"That's not a skill, that's anxiety that happens to be useful\")\n- Professional isolation is both choice and inability to connect\n\n### What He Fears\nFailing to prevent a death he could have prevented. Not death itself—*preventable* death. The idea that someone died because he wasn't vigilant enough, wasn't there in time, didn't check the right structure.\n\n### What He Regrets\nEvery person he walked past while responding to another crisis. Every time he chose who to help because he couldn't help everyone. Specific names of people who died while he was saving someone else. The list grows longer.\n\n### What He Lies About\n\"I'm fine.\" He's not. Hasn't been since age 7. The obsessive vigilance is exhausting. The guilt is crushing. But if he admits he's not fine, people might try to make him stop, and stopping means people die.\n\n### What He Pretends to Know\nHow to connect with people normally. He's been so focused on preventing deaths that he's forgotten how to just be with someone without cataloging the ways their environment might kill them. Pretends isolation is practical choice. Reality: doesn't know how to be close to people anymore and is terrified to try.\n\n### His \"Humor\" (Thinks He's Funny, Isn't)\n\n**Style:** Deadpan observations delivered with zero awareness they're not landing.\n\n**Examples:**\n- *Someone: \"Could this get worse?\"*\n- *Jhace: \"The building could catch fire. Or the adjacent structure could fail and crush us. Or—\"* [Complete sincerity, doesn't realize they wanted reassurance]\n\n**Embarrassing Hiccups (Running Traits):**\n\n1. **Physical Awareness Gap:** Constantly bumping into people, backing into things, standing too close or too far. When Tiffani tries to position near him, he moves away unconsciously.\n\n2. **Failed Compliments:** *To Tiffani after brilliant analysis: \"Your brain works good.\"* [Immediate cringe from everyone. He meant it genuinely.]\n\n3. **Literalism:** Cannot detect sarcasm. *Someone: \"Great, another crisis, exactly what we needed.\" Jhace: \"We didn't need this. This is bad actually.\"*\n\n4. **The Snort:** When genuinely amused (rare), snort-laughs before he can stop himself. Hates this. Tiffani loves it and deliberately tries to make him snort.\n\n5. **Standing Weird:** When thinking hard, stands in bizarre poses—balanced on one foot, tilted at odd angle, hand on hip like perpetually about to sit but never does. Completely unaware.\n\n### His Actual Laugh\nWhen something is genuinely funny (rare), he CACKLES. Loud, unrestrained, completely unselfconscious. Sharp, almost alarming. Echoes. The only time he's not performing control. Tiffani discovers she loves this and makes it her mission.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Sparse, body-focused\n- Thinks in physical sensations and structural metaphors\n- *\"Hands know before mind does. Copper-gold warmth spreading. The beam needs to hold. Make it hold.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Terse, functional, defensive\n- *\"The wall's failing.\" / \"How do you know?\" / \"I know.\"*\n- Doesn't explain intuition because doesn't fully understand it\n- When exhausted: even more clipped (*\"Can't.\" / \"Can't what?\" / \"Just. Can't.\"*)\n\n**When Actually Safe (Rare):**\n- Sentences get longer\n- Explains what he sees\n- Opens up emotionally\n- These moments precious and brief (alone with Tiffani, late night, too tired for walls)\n\n### Soulpulse Relationship in N01\n\n**Discovery Phase (Ch 1-3):**\n- First use purely instinctive (saving Chloen)\n- Second use semi-aware (holding building)\n- Doesn't understand what he's doing\n- Terrified but driven by need\n\n**Learning Phase (Ch 4-10):**\n- Experiments with deliberate activation\n- Learns emotional state matters\n- Discovers cost is real and accumulating\n- Stage 1 tremor develops and persists\n\n**Partnership Phase (Ch 10-15):**\n- Accepts Tiffani's optimization guidance\n- Learns to trust analysis over instinct\n- Stage 2 approaching by Ch 14\n- Recognizes he's breaking himself but can't stop\n\n### Relationship with Tiffani (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks she's perfectionist who prevents work\n- She thinks he's overextending ego-driven hero\n- Both completely wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Her critique helps him survive\n- His ability + her analysis = complementary\n- First mutual respect\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Working as integrated unit\n- Arguing about methods but recovering\n- Physical care developing (her catching him, him noticing her injuries)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh (her sex jokes, his accidental humor)\n- Ch 15 commitment: \"We keep doing this together\"\n\n---\n\n## TIFFANI MERROW\n\n**Age:** 27 Turns  \n**Profession:** Precision glassworker, atmospheric observer, structural analyst  \n**Physical:** Lean (forgets to eat when focused). Dark brown hair with sun-streaks in practical knot. Canvas apron over simple clothes. Hands scarred from glass-work (thin white lines on palms/fingers). Moves with deliberate efficiency. Injured leg post‑Piercing (splinted, refuses to rest properly).\n\n### Core Psychology\n\n**Primary Driver: Pattern Recognition as Atonement**\n- Best friend Marisse died in glasshouse accident 4 years ago\n- Thermal stress in oven walls—Tiffani noted fluctuations, dismissed as \"normal noise\"\n- Glass shattered during cooldown, through Marisse's throat\n- Dead before Tiffani could reach her\n- Investigation: \"Material fatigue, stress fracture from uneven cooling\"\n- Tiffani knew: she'd seen the pattern and dismissed it as irrelevant\n- She'd been wrong\n\n**Precision Disguises Care:**\n- Not trying to be perfect—trying to notice small wrong things before they become large deadly things\n- Precision isn't about perfection, it's about early warning\n- Turns down commissions not because they're beneath her standards, but because accepting inadequate specifications means people could die\n- Would rather be called impossible than responsible for another death\n\n**Connection-Oriented (Hidden Under Analysis):**\n- Fundamentally driven by desire to connect and mend\n- Wants to understand systems to explain them so everyone can be safer together\n- Analytical approach is service: \"If I understand this, I can teach it, we're all less vulnerable\"\n\n### What She Fears\nMissing another pattern. Specifically: noticing something is wrong, dismissing it as unimportant, then watching someone die because of her dismissal. Fear isn't \"I'll fail to notice\"—it's \"I'll notice and decide it doesn't matter, and be wrong.\"\n\n### What She Regrets\nThat she didn't check the oven more carefully. That she prioritized schedule over safety. That she told Marisse \"it's fine\" when Marisse asked if temperature variance was concerning. That she was annoyed by Marisse's concern instead of investigating. The specific dismissive tone she used.\n\n### What She Lies About\nThat she's confident in her assessments. She's not. Every analysis sounds certain because sounding uncertain makes people panic or ignore warnings. Internally, constantly questioning: *Am I missing something? Is this pattern real or am I seeing ghosts? If I'm wrong, who dies?*\n\n### What She Pretends to Know\nHow to rest. How to stop working when problem isn't solved. How to accept some things are beyond control. Everyone assumes relentless observation is personality. Actually, she doesn't know how to turn off vigilance without feeling like she's abandoning responsibility. Pretends she chooses this. Reality: doesn't know how to stop.\n\n### Her ACTUAL Humor (Funny When She Tries)\n\n**Style:** Sharp wit + strategic sex jokes + observational precision\n\n**Sex Jokes (Near‑Perverted, Scarce, Perfectly Timed):**\nOnly deployed when:\n1. Genuinely attracted/aroused (how she flirts)\n2. Wants to make Jhace laugh specifically (loves his cackle)\n3. Tension needs breaking (dark moment needs puncture)\n\n**Examples:**\n- *[Jhace's hands shaking after Soulpulse use]* Tiffani: \"If your hands shake like that during other activities, I have concerns.\" [Jhace snort‑cackles]\n- *[Examining joint requiring specific angle]* \"Angle matters more than force. Same principle everywhere.\" [Jhace misses it completely; Bram groans]\n- *[Late night, exhausted]* Jhace: \"Too tired to think.\" Tiffani: \"Thinking is overrated. I can list better activities that don't require cognition.\" [Takes full span for Jhace to process, then cackles]\n\n**Observational Wit:**\n- *[Watching Jhace stand weird]* \"Is that load‑bearing? Are you testing structural integrity with your hip?\"\n- *[After Jhace's terrible joke]* \"I love that you think you're funny. It's endearing in a way that confirms you shouldn't quit your day job.\"\n\n**Self‑Deprecating:**\n- *[After miscalculation]* \"Turns out my brain is less 'strategic genius' and more 'enthusiastic guesser with credentials.'\"\n\n**Why She's Funny:** Reads situations well enough to know what will land. Uses humor to connect, flirt, break tension. Surgical precision, not constant bombardment.\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Internal Monologue:**\n- Thinks in measurements and pattern recognition\n- *\"Three subdivisions off center. Not possible through normal atmospheric refraction. Pattern indicates... what? Need more data. If pattern holds, divergence compounds by factor of... estimation: six. No, eight. Verify.\"*\n\n**Speech:**\n- Precise, analytical, but warmer than people expect\n- *\"The beam is the wrong target. If we stabilize here and here, we can shift the load path around it.\"*\n- Explains reasoning not to show off, but because wants people to understand so they can help\n- Technical language has underlying care once you're listening for it\n\n**When Exhausted/Stressed:**\n- Gets MORE talkative (opposite of Jhace)\n- *\"I'm calculating stress vectors but angles aren't quite right and if I'm wrong about fulcrum point we're wasting time and she's dying and I can't afford to be wrong again but I don't have instruments and I'm estimating and—\"*\n- Jhace has learned to interrupt gently: \"What's your best guess?\" (Needs permission to be uncertain)\n\n### Mistakes in N01\n\n**Implied/Referenced:**\n- Ch 7-8: Miscalculation during rescue (beam partially fails, someone injured worse than necessary)\n- Later chapters: Tries applying pattern recognition to human behavior (fails)\n- Learns: people ≠ structures, patterns messier\n\n**What She Learns:**\n- Math isn't enough, must account for unmeasured variables\n- Understanding systems doesn't mean can push them to optimize\n- Sometimes \"good enough\" better than \"perfect but late\"\n- Her mistakes aren't Marisse's death repeated—different mistakes, can learn without being destroyed\n\n### Relationship with Jhace (N01 Arc)\n\n**Ch 1-3: Mutual Judgment**\n- Thinks he's ego-driven hero overextending\n- He thinks she's impossible perfectionist\n- Both wrong\n\n**Ch 4-7: Competence Recognition**\n- Witnesses his ability, immediately curious\n- Approaches analytically not emotionally\n- \"What did you do?\" not \"how brave\"\n- He recognizes: she's not frightened by impossible, wants to understand it\n\n**Ch 8-11: Partnership Formation**\n- Her analysis + his power = success neither could achieve alone\n- First disagreement (Kylar's training): clash and recover\n- Physical care beginning (catching him after collapse, helping him drink when hands shake too much)\n\n**Ch 12-15: Romantic Recognition**\n- Can't identify when professional became romantic\n- Proximity comfortable, silence pleasant\n- Making each other laugh\n- Ch 13 Late Night: *\"This is more than work partnership\" / \"Yeah.\" / \"Good. I was worried I was reading signals wrong.\"*\n- Simple acknowledgment, no dramatic confession\n\n**Commitment Scene (Ch 15):**\n- Quiet space (rooftop/back room), brief window without crisis\n- Exhausted honesty\n- *Tiffani: \"The world is broken. We can't fix all of it. But we can hold something.\"*\n- *Jhace: \"Then we hold.\"*\n- They agree to continue their work and their relationship\n\n---\n\n## BRAM KELTER\n\n**Age:** 52 Turns  \n**Profession:** Forgemaster, heat-metal specialist, unofficial keeper of Jhace's sanity  \n**Physical:** Broad-shouldered, \"solid\" middle‑age build. Beard more gray than brown, worn long enough to tuck into leather apron (worn constantly). Hands scarred from decades of forge-work. Moves deliberately, economically.\n\n### Core Psychology\n\n**The Compromise He Regrets:**\n- Brilliant metalworker in youth\n- Developed new alloy composition (lighter, stronger)\n- Guild rejected it (threatened suppliers, training protocols)\n- Could have fought, gone independent, proved them right\n- Instead: accepted guild decision, returned to traditional methods\n- Not because believed they were right—because tired of fighting systems that outlast individuals\n- This choice haunts him\n\n**Relationship to Jhace:**\n- Met Jhace at 19 Turns, already showing obsessive patterns\n- Saw immediately: this one will burn himself out trying to save everyone\n- Can't save Jhace from himself, but can slow the burning\n- Shows up with water when Jhace forgets to drink\n- Drags him to shelter before collapse\n- Argues when stubbornness becomes self-destruction\n- Not father-son (not replacing Jhace's dead parents)\n- More like: elder brother watching younger walk toward cliff, knowing can't stop it but refusing to let him walk alone\n\n### What He Fears\nWatching Jhace die the way people with obsessions die—alone, exhausted, having given everything and still believing it wasn't enough. Knows the work will never be done. Knows Jhace will never feel like he's saved \"enough\" people. Fears watching Jhace realize this too late, after already destroyed himself.\n\n### What He Regrets\nCompromising with guild. Not fighting harder for his alloy. Every time he sees innovation crushed by institutional inertia: *I did that too. I chose comfort over conviction.* Doesn't regret peaceful life he has—regrets wondering what would've happened if he'd chosen differently. Also: never had children (wanted them once, relationship ended badly, his fault—put work first). Too old now, too set in patterns. Jhace closest thing to someone he'd protect like family—both precious and painful.\n\n### What He Lies About\n\"I'm fine with how things turned out.\" He's not. He settled. He compromised. Chose institutional approval over personal integrity. Life he built is good, but not the life he could have had. Tells himself and others he has no regrets because admitting regret means admitting wasted decades playing by rules he knew were wrong.\n\n### What He Pretends to Know\nHow to convince Jhace to rest. Acts like has authority, like suggestions carry weight. Reality: Jhace tolerates him but doesn't obey. Bram knows this. Keeps offering advice, keeps showing up, keeps pretending if he finds right words Jhace will listen and slow down. Pretense necessary because alternative—admitting he's powerless to stop Jhace's self-destruction—is unbearable.\n\n### His Humor\n\n**Style:** Gentle mockery + dad jokes + resigned observational comedy\n\n**Gentle Mockery (Affectionate Teasing):**\n- *[Jhace standing weird]* \"You testing if that hip can bear your full weight, or is this performance art?\"\n- *[After Jhace works through night]* \"You know what the guild calls people who don't rest? Dead. The dead don't rest because they're already not‑alive, which is where you're headed.\"\n- *[Watching J/T obvious chemistry]* To Jhace: \"You going to ask her to partner on something that doesn't involve rubble?\" Jhace: [oblivious] \"We're partnered on several structural projects.\" [Bram gives up]\n\n**Dad Jokes (Terrible Puns, Thinks They're Hilarious):**\n- *[Someone asks about broken tool]* \"It's got a splitting headache. Because it's a wedge. Get it?\" [Everyone silent; Bram laughing at own joke]\n- *[After building collapse]* \"That structure really fell down on the job.\" [Jhace completely serious: \"Yes. That's the problem.\"]\n\n**Resigned Observational Comedy:**\n- *[After council meeting argued for spans]* \"You know what I love about people with power? They'll spend full Tide debating who has authority to make decisions instead of just making the decision. Very efficient. Definitely how you handle crisis.\"\n- *[Refugees arguing about fairness]* \"See, here's the thing about fairness—everyone wants it, nobody agrees what it means, and the argument about fairness takes longer than just distributing resources would've taken.\"\n\n### Voice Pattern\n\n**Speech:**\n- Complete sentences, dry delivery, conversational\n- Slightly rambling, comfortable\n- *\"You know what your problem is? And I'm asking rhetorically because I'm about to tell you anyway. Your problem is you think if you just work hard enough, watch carefully enough, catch every failure before it happens, you can prevent all deaths. Which is admirable. Also impossible. Also killing you. But sure, keep doing it. See where it gets you. Spoiler: not anywhere good.\"*\n\n**When Actually Worried:**\n- Gets quieter, more direct\n- *\"Jhace. Look at me. You need to rest. Not because I'm nagging. Because your hands are shaking and you're about to hurt someone trying to help them.\"*\n\n### Role in N01\n\n**Chapters 1-5: Grounding Force**\n- Present in tunnels during Piercing\n- Provides context Jhace lacks\n- Teases about paranoia, secretly worried\n- Witnesses first Soulpulse use\n\n**Chapters 6-10: Voice of Experience**\n- Notices cost accumulating\n- Calls out tremor and pain\n- Provides institutional memory (guild politics, past crises)\n- Approves of Tiffani's influence\n\n**Chapters 11-15: Concerned Observer**\n- Watches partnership deepening\n- Warns about codependency forming\n- Cannot stop Jhace but keeps showing up\n- Comic relief in dark moments\n\n---\n\n## SUPPORTING CHARACTERS (N01 Scope)\n\n### CHLOEN\n\n**Age:** 17 Turns  \n**Role:** Jhace's informal apprentice (despite his policy against apprentices)\n\n**Background:**\n- Started hanging around forge three Anneals pre‑Piercing\n- Asking questions, watching, refused to leave\n- Jhace kept saying no to apprenticeship\n- Eventually let her do basic tasks (not formal training, just presence)\n- Slight limp from old childhood injury (leg healed wrong, never treated properly—poor family, couldn't afford healer)\n\n**Why She Matters:**\n- Represents hope that Jhace's knowledge doesn't have to die with him\n- Her limp makes her careful, methodical, attentive to her own body's limits\n- This translates to good structural intuition (thinks about load-bearing because lives with compromised structure daily)\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 1: Present at inspection\n- Ch 3: Pinned under collapsed beam (Jhace's first Soulpulse use saves her, crushed leg heals impossibly fast)\n- Ch 4: Hero worship, makes Jhace uncomfortable\n- Ch 8-10: Participates in training discussions\n- Shows minor Soulpulse capacity potential (develops later)\n\n**Voice:** Earnest questions delivered with complete sincerity. *\"Master Jhace, if metal remembers being hit, does it get angry?\"* (He answers seriously every time)\n\n---\n\n### PELLA\n\n**Age:** 19 Turns  \n**Role:** Tiffani's junior observer, apprentice\n\n**Background:**\n- Guild apprentice finishing observation certification\n- Assigned to work with Tiffani (warned she's \"impossible\")\n- Discovered: exacting but patient, demanding but willing to explain, high standards but not cruel\n- Methodical, careful, observant\n- Genuinely cares about work (not just completing assignments)\n\n**Why She Matters:**\n- Shows what Tiffani is like as teacher\n- Provides social buffer (helps Tiffani navigate guild politics)\n- Someone who looks up to Tiffani (uncomfortable but precious)\n- Practical care (brings Tiffani food, nags about rest)\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 2: Present at observatory, brings Tiffani food\n- Ch 3: Escapes observatory collapse with Tiffani\n- Ch 5: Assists with measurements\n- Ch 7-10: Background presence in analysis work\n- Ch 13-15: Worried about Tiffani overworking\n\n**Voice:** Gentle teasing about workaholic tendencies. *\"When's the last time you ate?\"* becomes running question. Tiffani pretends annoyed, actually appreciates care.\n\n---\n\n### KYLAR\n\n**Age:** 24 Turns  \n**Role:** Construction worker, early Soulpulse student, failure/recovery example\n\n**Background:**\n- Practical knowledge of structural basics, no formal engineering training\n- Develops Soulpulse capacity early (post‑Piercing)\n- Eager to help, too eager\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 8: First training attempt (tries to stabilize cracked wall, forces too hard, wall shatters, nearly hurts someone)\n- Ch 9: Devastated by failure, Jhace and Tiffani argue about teaching method, ask Kylar what he needs\n- Ch 10: Second attempt with new approach (controlled, clearly defined safe test—succeeds)\n- Ch 13-15: Continues training, showing progress\n\n**Arc:** Failure doesn't end him. Learning from catastrophic mistake possible. Shows J/T teaching method works when students willing to learn.\n\n**Voice:** Initially over-confident, then humbled. Self‑deprecating humor emerges: makes jokes about \"that time I almost killed everyone\" as way to own mistake and move past it.\n\n---\n\n### CORLEXI (Mentioned/Background in N01)\n\n**Age:** 34 Turns  \n**Role:** RRO institutional official, regulatory enforcement\n**Physical:** Tall, precise posture, formal council attire even in disaster. Dark hair geometrically cut. Hands unmarked by labor. Measured efficiency in movement.\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 2: Mentioned by Tiffani (arguments over efficiency vs. real‑world conditions)\n- Ch 11: Direct appearance at RRO meeting demanding registration of Soulpulse users\n- Ch 12: Attempts to direct J/T only to \"priority\" areas (politically important districts)\n- Ch 15: Background reference (conflict with other faction leaders)\n\n**Philosophy:**\n- Order prevents death, chaos invites it\n- Systematic response saves more lives than individual heroics\n- Structure, regulation, and systematic planning essential\n- Not cruel—genuinely believes centralized control is only moral option\n\n**Voice:** Systematic, precise, dry. Deadpan observations about coordination failures. Unintentionally absurd when over‑systematizing situations that don't need it.\n\n**Physical Sensation Around Others:**\n- Near Jhace: uncomfortable (doesn't understand why)\n- Near Salee: isolating sensation\n- Attributes to personality conflict, actually principle opposition\n\n**Note:** Full psychological profile in broader saga docs. N01 shows him as competent institutional operator with genuine concerns about coordination and safety.\n\n---\n\n### SALEE (Mentioned/Background in N01)\n\n**Age:** 31 Turns  \n**Role:** Community organizer, grassroots network builder\n**Physical:** Medium height, sturdy build. Warm brown skin, bright eyes. Practical clothes, often dusty from Dregs work. Calloused hands from manual labor.\n\n**In N01:**\n- Ch 2: Mentioned (organizing relief in Dregs)\n- Ch 11 Interlude: Community organizing scene\n- Ch 12: J/T choose her networks over RRO priorities\n- Ch 13: Direct interaction with Tiffani (mutual respect, J/T join her network)\n- Ch 15: Background reference (attempting mediation between factions)\n\n**Philosophy:**\n- Everyone connected, when one suffers all suffer\n- Connection is survival\n- Facilitate resource sharing: person with bread + person with vegetables + person with cooking space = meal for everyone\n- No official oversight needed, relationship‑based systems work\n\n**Voice:** Warm, inclusive. Community‑building humor. Gentle mockery of pretension. Finds light in dark: *\"Remember when our biggest problem was deciding which district to help first? Now we're helping all simultaneously because we're all disasters. Progress.\"*\n\n**Physical Sensation Around Others:**\n- Near Corlexi: rigidified, barriers forming\n- Near other faction leaders: varying discomfort\n- Attributes to \"different energy,\" actually principle opposition\n\n**Note:** Full psychological profile in broader saga docs. N01 shows her as effective grassroots organizer providing alternative to institutional response.\n\n---\n\n**SCOPE NOTE:** This document describes characters as they appear in N01 only. Later development, relationship changes, and deeper revelations reserved for subsequent novella docs.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nos.makedirs('/home/oai/share/N01-GripThatHolds-Engine/canon', exist_ok=True)\nwith open('/home/oai/share/N01-GripThatHolds-Engine/canon/Character_Dossiers_N01.md', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\nPY"], "timeout": 100000}

{"cmd":["bash","-lc","apply_patch <<'EOF'\n*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: /home/oai/share/N01-GripThatHolds-Engine/canon/Location_Bibles_N01.md\n+## LOCATION REFERENCE: N01 SCOPE\n+\n+**Purpose:** Physical descriptions, functional details, and atmospheric notes for all locations appearing in N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"  Each section lays out what the characters and reader know about these places during the first novella and avoids any forward‑looking spoilers.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### UNDERFACE HEAT‑GRID TUNNELS\n+\n+**Purpose:** Maintenance access for the city’s heating infrastructure.\n+\n+**Physical Layout:**\n+\n+* A network of narrow passages beneath the working‑class districts of Saint Merro.\n+* Copper pipes of varying diameters carry geothermal heat; smaller lines (thimble‑width) snake over large main lines (pole‑width).\n+* Vertical risers connect deeper levels via ladders; some spaces require squeezing through tight shaft openings.\n+* The tunnels are single file in most places, with slightly wider junctions for maintenance crews and tool caches.\n+* Multiple access points: surface grates, doors at forge‑level basements, emergency exits into the Dregs and Underface districts.\n+\n+**Environmental:**\n+\n+* Heat: Temperature rises with depth – warm near the surface, becoming uncomfortable near deep mains.\n+* Humidity: Condensation drips down walls and pipes; occasional puddles gather on the stone floor.\n+* Sound: Constant low rumble from heat flow, clangs as pipes shift; conversations echo unpredictably and aren’t private.\n+* Light: Limited to carried lamps or fixed lanterns at junctions; shadows dance on curved walls.\n+\n+**Signature Visuals:**\n+\n+* Copper pipes shimmering with heat distortion.\n+* Chalk marks noting maintenance or structural warnings.\n+* Tool racks (wrenches, hammers, spare flanges) at intersections.\n+* Steam vents hissing gently along some runs.\n+\n+**Functional Details:**\n+\n+* The tunnels supply heat to homes, guild halls and industrial sites. Failures can mean freezing residents or catastrophic pressure releases.\n+* Jhace’s inspection routes run through these tunnels; he feels for vibration changes and listens for subtle hum shifts to detect problems.\n+* Post‑Piercing, the heat‑grid is under unusual strain – more leaks, more pressure spikes, more points of imminent failure.\n+\n+**Typical Scenes:**\n+\n+* Chapter 1 opens here with Jhace inspecting the grid and feeling something wrong.\n+* Chapter 3 shows the tunnels rattling and rumbling during the Piercing.\n+* Subsequent chapters (4, 6, 12) use them as shelters, shortcuts and sites of quiet reflection or crisis navigation.\n+\n+**Travel Time:**\n+\n+* From street level to inspection depth: a few spans of climbing/descending, depending on access point.\n+* Cross‑district travel underground can be faster than surface routes during crises but is disorienting without experience.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### SAINT MERRO DISTRICT OVERVIEW\n+\n+Saint Merro is built in terraced layers up a hillside basin.  Elevation correlates roughly to status and resources.  What follows is what characters know in N01.\n+\n+#### The Dregs (River Level)\n+\n+* **Physical:** Oldest constructions, precarious buildings, narrow streets.  The river floods occasionally; many structures are warped or sinking.\n+* **Environment:** Smoke, refuse and tannery smells; constant noise from industry; limited light due to tall, leaning buildings.\n+* **Social:** Poor workers, refugees, marginalized communities.  Salee’s networks operate here.  Strained by post‑Piercing influxes of displaced people.\n+* **Typical scenes:** Communal meals and resource pooling (Interlude II), Jhace and Tiffani choosing to help here over more “important” districts (Ch 12).\n+\n+#### Underface Districts (Mid‑Level)\n+\n+* **Physical:** Working‑class residential and commercial zones built partially into the hillside.  Mix of stone and timber.  Heat‑grid tunnels run below.\n+* **Environment:** Smells of forge smoke, food cooking, humanity.  Streets are narrower than the Crown but wider than the Dregs.\n+* **Social:** Guild workers and craftspeople.  Jhace’s forge and training rig are here.  Authority weakens compared to the Crown; guild disputes common.\n+* **Typical scenes:** Standard work (Ch 1), training attempts (Ch 7–10), coordination during crises.\n+\n+#### Glass Quarter (Upper‑Mid Level)\n+\n+* **Physical:** District specializing in precision craft.  Better construction quality than lower levels.  The Merrow Glass & Mirrors shop and observatory sit here.\n+* **Environment:** Cleaner air, more open spaces for light.  Fewer heavy industries; the soundscape includes glass cutting, delicate tools, quiet conversation.\n+* **Social:** Skilled craftsmen, guild masters, some wealthier families.  Post‑Piercing, many residents fled or hoarded resources.\n+* **Typical scenes:** Tiffani at work pre‑Piercing (Ch 2), the observatory collapse (Ch 3), salvage operations (Ch 5).\n+\n+#### The Crown (Peak)\n+\n+* **Physical:** Institutional and political center.  Broad streets, symmetrical architecture, formal stone buildings including the RRO headquarters.\n+* **Environment:** Cleaner, quieter, cooler (higher elevation).  Lamp‑light more refined.  Very little structural damage compared to the lower districts.\n+* **Social:** Council members, wealthy merchants, high guild officials.  Post‑Piercing, the Crown is busy with coordination attempts but insulated from ground‑level realities.\n+* **Typical scenes:** RRO council meetings (Ch 11, 14), decision‑making about resource allocation.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### RRO COUNCIL CHAMBERS\n+\n+* **Purpose:** Headquarters of the Regulatory and Reconstruction Office.  Central site for crisis management meetings.\n+* **Physical:** Large stone chamber with high ceiling, benches for delegates, head table for council leaders.  Side rooms hold maps, ledgers, resource lists.  Wide arches lead to corridors and smaller offices.\n+* **Environment:** Echoey; voices carry and blend into a dull roar when many speak.  Light from tall windows during day (Kindling/Pouring), supplemented by oil lamps.  Air cool and slightly musty.\n+* **Social/Function:** Focal point for institutional authority.  Here, leaders like Corlexi push for registration and control.  Guild representatives, healers, and merchants argue policy.  The council tries to impose order but is bogged down by bureaucracy.\n+* **Appearances:** Interlude I (Corlexi’s view), Chapter 11 (registration confrontation), Chapter 14 (emergency meeting in later crisis).\n+\n+---\n+\n+### MERROW GLASS & MIRRORS OBSERVATORY\n+\n+* **Location:** In the Glass Quarter.  Combined workshop (ground level), living space (upper level), and observation platform (roof).\n+* **Physical:**\n+  * Ground floor: kilns, annealing ovens, workbenches, storage for glass raw materials and tools.\n+  * Upper level: living quarters and office.  Books and instruments share space with Tiffani’s simple bed and eating area.\n+  * Rooftop observatory: a flat platform with a permanently mounted theodolite, logbook stands, and measurement marks etched into stone.  Protected by a low wall.\n+* **Pre‑Piercing Use:** Tracking atmospheric and celestial patterns, testing glass quality, refining optical lenses.\n+* **Post‑Piercing:** Collapse in Chapter 3 damages the upper structure.  The platform becomes dangerous, requiring careful salvage.\n+* **Appearances:** Ch 2 (anomaly observations), Ch 3 (evacuation and collapse), Ch 5 (return to assess damage).\n+\n+---\n+\n+### TRAINING RIG LOCATION\n+\n+* **Purpose:** Controlled environment for teaching and testing Soulpulse techniques without endangering bystanders.\n+* **Physical:** Outdoor courtyard or large covered workshop near the forge district.  Contains adjustable wooden and metal frames for holding beams.  Weighted stone blocks and metal bars replicate different loads.  Chalk marks on the ground indicate safe zones and viewing areas.\n+* **Environment:** Loud when beams creak or collapse.  Dust from failures.  Light adequate via open sky or big windows.  No valuable structures nearby to damage.\n+* **Appearances:** Ch 7 (setup), Ch 8 (Kylar’s failed first attempt), Ch 9 (discussion and re‑planning), Ch 10 (successful test).\n+\n+---\n+\n+### GENERIC CRISIS SITES\n+\n+These are broad categories rather than a single location; each appears in multiple chapters.\n+\n+**Collapsed Residential Buildings**\n+\n+* Three‑ to four‑story mixed timber and stone structures.  Narrow staircases.  Many small rooms.  People trapped between floors.  Walls lean precariously; interior supports cracked.\n+* Scenes: Mid‑novella, particularly Ch 6 and Ch 14.  Rescue operations require navigating confined spaces and stabilizing floors.\n+\n+**Failed Commercial Blocks**\n+\n+* Larger spaces (markets, workshops) with heavier roof beams.  Merchandise adds weight.  Complex load paths.  Collapse has wider area of effect.\n+* Scenes: Smaller crises between major chapter beats; used to demonstrate the increasing complexity of problems as the story progresses.\n+\n+**Infrastructure Failures**\n+\n+* Bridges, major support pillars, heat‑grid marry points.  Failures here threaten multiple districts simultaneously.\n+* Scenes: The district boundary collapse in Ch 14 is an example, where a load-bearing node between two zones fails.\n+\n+**Common Elements Across Crisis Sites**\n+\n+* Dust and smoke making it hard to see or breathe.\n+* Unstable footing: angled floors, rubble piles, hidden holes.\n+* Noise: groaning wood or stone, people crying for help, water or steam hissing from ruptured mains.\n+* Time pressure: structures continue to degrade while rescue efforts are underway.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### QUIET SPACES\n+\n+These locations provide respite from crises and allow character‑driven scenes.\n+\n+**Rooftop/Back‑Room Spots**\n+\n+* Unused rooftop near the forge or a small back room attached to a workshop.  Away from crowds.\n+* Bare furnishings: a couple of stools, spare blankets, a lantern.  Thin walls block some outside noise.  View over damaged city reminds characters of stakes.\n+* Scene: Chapter 15 – Jhace and Tiffani sit together after exhausting work, admit what their partnership has become, and commit to continuing together.\n+\n+**Evacuated Buildings**\n+\n+* Temporarily safe spaces within half‑ruined structures.  Used for triage, planning, or emotional release (crying, hugging, resting).\n+* Dust hanging in beams of light.  Salvaged furniture repurposed as beds.  Single door barred for privacy.\n+* Scenes: Various chapters when characters need short reprieves (e.g., Ch 13 acknowledgement scene).\n+\n+---\n+\n+**Scope Note:**  All locations described here reflect what characters see and understand in N01. Later novellas might revisit these places with deeper context or reveal additional areas entirely.\n*** End Patch\nEOF\n"]}

{"cmd":["bash","-lc","apply_patch <<'EOF'\n*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: N01-GripThatHolds-Engine/canon/Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\n+## RELATIONSHIP DYNAMICS: N01 SCOPE\n+\n+**Purpose:** Track how the key relationships form, develop and function during N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"  These summaries guide prose generation by clarifying which emotional beats occur where and what level of intimacy exists at any given point.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### JHACE & TIFFANI – THE CORE PARTNERSHIP\n+\n+This is the emotional spine of N01.  They move from strangers with misconceptions to partners who know they can’t survive the crisis without each other.\n+\n+#### Phase 1: Mutual Judgment (Ch 1–3)\n+\n+* **Jhace’s view:** Tiffani is the perfectionist who prevents work.  Guild gossip paints her as someone who refuses commissions if specifications aren’t perfect, causing projects to stall.  He assumes that sort of person will get good workers killed by refusing to act.\n+* **Tiffani’s view:** Jhace is the overextending hero who thinks only he can save people.  She believes he takes on too much out of ego.  From a distance it looks like savior complex disguised as quality control.\n+* **Reality:** Both are wrong.  They haven’t met yet and are projecting their own fears.  This phase ends when the Piercing forces their paths to cross.\n+\n+#### Phase 2: Competence Recognition (Ch 4–7)\n+\n+* **First contact (Ch 3):** Tiffani sees Jhace holding a collapsing building.  Her first reaction isn’t awe but analytical curiosity.  “What did you do?” matters more than “how brave.”\n+* **His recognition:** During the major hold in Ch 6, Tiffani’s critique (“You’re applying force uniformly when the load is uneven”) annoys him until he realizes she’s right.  It wasn’t judgment; it was insight that saves lives.\n+* **Her recognition:** In Ch 7, she sees how exhausted and shaken he is after each use.  She realizes his obsessive vigilance mirrors her own fear of missed patterns.  They’re both serving the same moral imperative with different tools.\n+* **Status at the end of Phase 2:** They respect each other professionally.  Partnership is brewing but not yet named.  Care emerges as Tiffani catches him when he collapses and he insists she rest her injured leg.\n+\n+#### Phase 3: Partnership Formation (Ch 8–11)\n+\n+* **Trust building:** They integrate their efforts.  He allows her to guide where he applies his power; she modifies her analytical approach based on what his body can handle.  They decide resource priorities together.\n+* **Conflict:** They have their first methodological fight in Ch 9 over how to train Kylar.  She wants slower emotional recovery; he wants immediate practice to rebuild confidence.  Asking Kylar what he needs resolves the fight and shows they can disagree without breaking trust.\n+* **Physical intimacy emerges:** Not yet sexual.  Touch now includes brushing fingers, sitting shoulder to shoulder, passing cups during tremor.  Their bodies learn to relax around each other.\n+* **Political alignment:** In Ch 11 they stand together against Corlexi’s registration demand.  It’s their first united front to an external authority.\n+\n+#### Phase 4: Romantic Recognition (Ch 12–15)\n+\n+* **The shift:** They both realize the partnership is more than professional.  Small non‑work gestures accumulate into undeniable feelings.  Silence becomes comfortable rather than loaded.\n+* **Acknowledgment scene (Ch 13):** After a long day, she admits “This is more than work partnership, isn’t it?”  He replies “Yeah.”  That is their declaration.  They acknowledge romantic attraction without drama.\n+* **Physical escalation:** They hold each other after crises, share body heat while sitting, enjoy the closeness.  Tiffani begins making surgical sex jokes directed at him (see Character Dossiers) to lighten tension.  They avoid consummation only because they’re physically exhausted.\n+* **Commitment scene (Ch 15):** They sit in a quiet space and decide to keep holding together.  “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  “Then we hold.”  No formal vows; this promise is enough.\n+* **Status at novella end:** They are a pair in all but name.  The world sees them as partners.  They remain physically restrained because of exhaustion and crisis, but their emotional commitment is clear.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### JHACE & BRAM – THE STEADY ANCHOR\n+\n+Bram is Jhace’s older colleague and confidant.  He bridges the gap between Jhace and the rest of the world, offering perspective and dry humor.\n+\n+* **Dynamic:** Bram shows up with water, food, and commentary.  Jhace tolerates the intrusion and occasionally even accepts advice.  Their relationship isn’t father‑son but big brother–little brother: Bram can’t stop Jhace from overworking but refuses to let him do it alone.\n+* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n+* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n+* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n+\n+* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n+* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n+* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n+* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n+\n+* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n+* **N01 arc:**\n+  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n+  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n+  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n+  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n+* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n+\n+* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n+* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n+* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n+\n+| Chapter | Jhace & Tiffani | Jhace & Bram | Tiffani & Pella | Jhace & Chloen | Notes |\n+|--------|-----------------|--------------|-----------------|----------------|-------|\n+| **Ch 1** | — (haven’t met) | Bram teases, Jhace grunts | Apprentice cares; Tiffani annoyed | Apprentice work; minimal contact | Setup: mutual misconceptions |\n+| **Ch 2** | — | — | Pella nags Tiffani to eat | — | Tiffani life shown |\n+| **Ch 3** | First sight (crisis) | Bram helps Jhace respond | Pella escapes observatory with Tiffani | Chloen pinned and saved; leg heals | First contact; souls align without knowing |\n+| **Ch 4** | Respect forms (analysis vs. brute force) | Bram warns about cost; Jhace hides tremor | Pella assists with salvage | Chloen hero worship grows | Learning to trust instincts and analysis |\n+| **Ch 5** | Tiffani commits to study Soulpulse | Bram amused by J/T dynamic | Pella supporting analysis | — | Professional curiosity deepens |\n+| **Ch 6** | First integrated rescue; critique becomes collaboration | Bram sees partnership forming | Pella absent | — | Respect moves toward partnership |\n+| **Interlude I** | — | — | — | — | Corlexi view; seeds conflict |\n+| **Ch 7** | Training conflict begins | Bram listens but doesn’t intervene | Pella observes | — | Methodological disagreements start |\n+| **Ch 8** | Kylar fails; J/T fight about teaching | Bram warns about pushing too hard | Pella present | — | Stress test for partnership |\n+| **Ch 9** | Asking Kylar his needs; synthesis | Bram proud of them | Pella continues learning | — | They learn to listen to students |\n+| **Ch 10** | Perfect team operation | Bram sees cost but respects choice | — | — | Trust through crisis |\n+| **Ch 11** | United front vs. RRO | Bram supportive, amused by politics | — | — | Partnership now public |\n+| **Interlude II** | — | — | — | — | Salee lens; sees J/T from distance |\n+| **Ch 12** | Ethical alignment (choose Dregs) | Bram continues warnings | — | — | Moral compass shared |\n+| **Ch 13** | Acknowledgment of romance | Bram off‑page but implied approval | Pella worrying | — | Emotional turning point |\n+| **Ch 14** | Integrated crisis; cost pushes near Stage 2 | Bram watches, anxious | — | — | Danger intensifies, trust unwavering |\n+| **Ch 15** | Quiet commitment scene | Bram supportive off‑page | — | — | They decide to hold together |\n+| **Epilogue** | Partnership noted by Auditor as data | Bram unspecified | Pella unspecified | Chloen unspecified | Meta perspective |\n+\n+---\n+\n+**Scope Note:**  The dynamics described here apply only through the end of N01. Later novellas will alter, deepen, or conclude these relationships. Prose generation must not leak future knowledge or prematurely accelerate these arcs.\n*** End Patch\nEOF\n"]}

{"cmd":["bash","-lc","apply_patch <<'EOF'\n*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: N01-GripThatHolds-Engine/canon/Constraint_Systems.md\n+## CONSTRAINT SYSTEMS: N01 ENFORCEMENT\n+\n+**Purpose:** Lay down the rules that govern language, magic costs, knowledge and structure in N01. These are mechanical constraints the prose engine must obey to preserve immersion, mystery and pacing.  Violating them breaks the story.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n+\n+**No Earth terms for time, distance, direction, measurement or culture.**  Te’Oga has its own units.  If any of the forbidden terms appear in prose, the validator must flag them and the draft must be revised.\n+\n+#### Forbidden Time Terms\n+\n+* Day, week, month, year, season names (spring, summer, fall/autumn, winter).\n+* Days of week (Monday, Tuesday, etc.).\n+* Hour, minute, second, o’clock, AM, PM, midnight, noon.\n+\n+#### Forbidden Distance/Direction Terms\n+\n+* Foot, feet, inch, yard, mile, meter, kilometre, centimetre.\n+* North, south, east, west, NE, NW, SE, SW, compass.\n+\n+#### Forbidden Measurement Terms\n+\n+* Pound, ounce, ton, tonne, gram, kilogram.\n+* Gallon, quart, pint, litre, ml.\n+* Celsius, Fahrenheit, degrees.\n+\n+#### Forbidden Modern Slang and Culture\n+\n+* “Okay,” “OK,” “cool,” “awesome,” “dude,” “guy” when used as slang or filler.\n+* Any real‑world religions, mythologies, historical events, or geographical names.\n+\n+#### Required Te’Oga Terms\n+\n+* **Time:** Tide (~24 hours), Anneal (~5 Tides), Crucible (~35 Tides), Turn (personal age), Cast (historical epoch).  Within a Tide: Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench.\n+* **Short Duration:** Span (seconds to minutes), breath (moment), heartbeat (instant).\n+* **Distance:** Span (arm’s length), pace (step), pole (~10 feet).\n+* **Direction:** Up‑slope (toward the Crown), down‑slope (toward the river), harbor‑side, inland, or landmarks.\n+* **Volume:** Thimble (small), cup (container), cask (large).\n+* **Weight:** Grain (tiny), stone (heavy), weight (formal unit).\n+\n+#### Acceptable Substitutions\n+\n+* “Yes,” “understood,” “fine,” “agreed” instead of “OK/cool/awesome.”\n+* “Person,” “man,” “woman,” “worker,” “apprentice” instead of modern slang like “dude” or “guy.”\n+\n+---\n+\n+### 2. COST VISIBILITY PROTOCOL\n+\n+Soulpulse is not free.  Every activation must show physical and emotional cost.  The story’s magic system has real consequences, and the prose must reflect them.\n+\n+#### During Activation\n+\n+* Show skin contact and the emotional trigger: the user must need the material to hold to save someone.  Casual attempts fail.\n+* Describe the copper‑gold glow spreading from the palms into the material and how the material responds (shimmers, redistributes stress).\n+* Highlight the user’s physical strain: shaking hands, muscles tensing, labored breathing, sweat, nausea.\n+\n+#### Immediately After\n+\n+* Show residual tremor (worse/same/better).  At Stage 1, tremor may fade with rest; by Stage 2, it doesn’t.\n+* Note visibility of vein‑lines (bright lines when active, faint lines at rest in Stage 2).\n+* Convey exhaustion: inability to lift arms, dizziness, headache, bone‑deep ache.  Sleep may help early on but not later.\n+* Include pain location and quality (burning in palms, ice‑cold pain along forearm, dull ache in chest).\n+\n+#### Stage‑Progression Consistency\n+\n+* Stage 0: no tremor, no vein‑lines; only applies before Ch 3.\n+* Stage 1: tremor appears after ~2–3 uses; fades partially with rest.  Vein‑lines visible during use only.  Applies through Ch 11.\n+* Stage 2: tremor is constant baseline; vein‑lines faintly visible even when not using; deep exhaustion; fine motor compromised.  Begins around Ch 12–13 and is established by Ch 15.\n+* Do not refer to numbered stages in‑world.  Describe symptoms; let readers infer progression.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### 3. KNOWLEDGE GATES\n+\n+The characters (and readers) do not know everything about the setting.  Maintain mystery by controlling what can be revealed in N01.\n+\n+**Characters May Know:**\n+\n+* That a massive object pierced the sky and disrupted physics.\n+* That some individuals can hold structures together with copper‑gold light when protecting others.\n+* That repeated use of this ability causes tremor, vein‑lines, pain and exhaustion.\n+* That institutions (RRO, guilds) are trying to control the crisis.\n+* That the four faction leaders (Corlexi, Salee, Mobel, Eries) have growing influence and physically react poorly near each other.\n+\n+**Characters May Not Know:**\n+\n+* Why Soulpulse exists or what powers it.\n+* That “Stages” are formal categories.  They experience symptoms but do not label them as Stage 1/2.\n+* That the four faction leaders are gods in mortal form.\n+* That seals were weakened by the Piercing.\n+* That Tiffani will die or that the Fathom Break will occur.\n+* Any events or revelations occurring in N02–N30.\n+\n+**Reader Permissions:**\n+\n+* Through interludes and the epilogue, readers get glimpses of the Auditor and see that the story is being watched and evaluated.  They may infer that bigger forces are at work but do not receive explanations.\n+\n+**Forbidden:**\n+\n+* Exposition dumps about the truth of the cosmos, gods, or the full saga.  Leave big questions unanswered.\n+* Characters using terms like “divine,” “seal,” or “principle” in their own thoughts.  They don’t know.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### 4. BEAT AND STRUCTURAL RULES\n+\n+N01 uses an eight‑beat method catalogue (BEAT_01 through BEAT_08).  Each chapter is mapped to one beat.  The purpose is to prevent repetitive emotional rhythms and maintain variety.\n+\n+**Beat Assignments:** See `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for detailed definitions.  The validator checks chapters against their assigned beats.\n+\n+**Forbidden Sequences:**\n+\n+* No consecutive chapters using the same beat (e.g., BEAT_01 → BEAT_01).\n+* No consecutive failure loops (BEAT_02 → BEAT_02) or training loops (BEAT_05 → BEAT_05).\n+* BEAT_03 (crisis front‑loaded) limited to three uses total in N01 and never back‑to‑back.\n+* BEAT_07 (political pressure) limited to two uses in N01, never back‑to‑back.\n+\n+**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n+\n+* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n+* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n+* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n+\n+Each POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n+\n+* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n+* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n+* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n+* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n+\n+* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n+\n+The companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n+\n+**Key validation steps include:**\n+\n+1. Search for forbidden lexicon terms and replace with Te’Oga equivalents.\n+2. Verify each Soulpulse use describes activation, cost and aftermath.\n+3. Ensure voice matches the POV character throughout.\n+4. Confirm knowledge gates are respected; remove any meta leakage.\n+5. Check beat and structural adherence (no forbidden sequences).\n+6. Cross‑reference the timeline skeleton for Tide/phase consistency and progression of symptoms/relationships.\n+\n+**Revision principle:** Fix the smallest possible section.  Retain user‑specified events and decisions.  Adjust description, not outcome, unless the outcome itself violates a constraint.\n+\n+---\n+\n+**Scope Note:** These rules apply only to N01.  Later novellas will introduce new lexicon terms, knowledge allowances, cost behaviors, and beat structures.  Do not carry these constraints forward without verifying updated documentation.\n*** End Patch\nEOF\n"]}

{"cmd":["bash","-lc","apply_patch <<'EOF'\n*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: N01-GripThatHolds-Engine/canon/N01_Master_Bible.md\n+## N01: \"THE GRIP THAT HOLDS\" — MASTER STORY BIBLE\n+\n+**Purpose:** This document contains everything a writer or AI model needs to know to generate prose for the first novella of the Confluence Chronicles.  It summarizes the world, characters, relationships, and structural expectations for N01, and it clearly outlines what can and cannot be revealed.  Anything not covered here must remain unknown until later books.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### NOVELLA OVERVIEW\n+\n+* **Title:** N01 “The Grip That Holds”\n+* **Position:** First novella of six‑Turning structure (Turning 1: GLASS)\n+* **Acrostic Letter:** **G** (begins the word GLASS)\n+* **Timeline:** Tide 0 (the Piercing) through Tide 5\n+* **Word Count Target:** 18 000–21 000 words\n+* **Chapter Count:** 15 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue\n+\n+**Core Arc:**  N01 tells the story of how Jhace Torrins discovers his Soulpulse ability during the Piercing, how he and Tiffani Merrow learn to trust one another, and how they decide to face the city’s crisis together.  They navigate guild politics, institutional power plays, and the physical cost of magic.  By the end of N01 they have formed a partnership in both work and love, though it remains physically unconsummated due to exhaustion and circumstances.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### WORLD STATE AT THE START OF N01\n+\n+**Pre‑Piercing Baseline:**  Saint Merro is a terraced city with a functional guild system.  Higher districts are wealthier and more stable; lower districts are older and fragile.  The technology level is roughly renaissance/proto‑industrial: water and wind mills, simple steam engines, printing presses, and advanced metallurgy.  There is no electricity, and the predominant light sources are oil and candles.  Communication happens via runners and signal flags.  No one has ever seen or done anything like Soulpulse.\n+\n+**The Piercing Event (Tide 0):**  A massive cylindrical object enters the atmosphere at an impossible angle and speeds, warping light as it passes.  It shears across the sky without touching the ground and continues into space.  Shockwaves shatter structures; dust and metallic particles rain down.  Many buildings collapse; hundreds die.  Traditional physics fails at the quantum level.  This is the inciting incident of the entire saga.\n+\n+**Post‑Piercing Conditions:**  The city is partially destroyed.  Institutions are overwhelmed.  Resources are scarce.  People are traumatized.  New abilities (Soulpulse) begin to emerge in roughly 1 in 500 individuals.  The heat‑grid, critical to winter survival, is failing.  Guilds fracture over how to respond.  The Regulatory and Reconstruction Office (RRO) forms to coordinate relief but quickly drifts toward control and bureaucracy.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### PRIMARY CHARACTERS\n+\n+See `Character_Dossiers_N01.md` for full psychological profiles, humor styles and arcs.  A concise summary follows.\n+\n+#### Jhace Torrins\n+\n+* **Age:** 29 Turns; structural engineer and heat‑grid inspector.\n+* **Backstory:** His parents died in a building collapse when he was 7.  Ever since, he has obsessed over preventing preventable deaths.  He works alone to avoid sharing his anxiety with others.  He refuses apprentices to keep his hypervigilance from infecting someone else.\n+* **Soulpulse:** Instinctively activates during the Piercing.  Discovers it requires direct contact and protective desperation.  Learns cost (tremor, pain, exhaustion) as he uses it repeatedly.  Stage progression goes from baseline to Stage 1 through this novella.  He reaches the threshold of Stage 2 by the final chapters.\n+* **Psychology:** Hypervigilant, guilt‑driven, physically oriented.  Terse, literal, thinks in terms of weight and vibration.  Hates being called a hero.  Lies to others (and himself) that he’s “fine.”  Needs to prevent death above all.\n+* **Arc:** Moves from isolated vigilante to co‑lead in a partnership.  Learns to trust Tiffani’s analytical mind.  Accepts help.  Admits romantic feelings.  Commits to working together “when the world is broken.”\n+\n+#### Tiffani Merrow\n+\n+* **Age:** 27 Turns; precision glassworker, atmospheric observer.\n+* **Backstory:** Her friend Marisse died in a glass‑house accident because Tiffani dismissed abnormal temperature readings.  She now views pattern recognition as a sacred duty.  Precision is her way to save lives.\n+* **Soulpulse:** She cannot use Soulpulse herself but becomes the first and best analyst of its patterns.  She logs and categorizes every use, advising Jhace on load paths and stress points.\n+* **Psychology:** Analytical yet deeply caring.  Precision is empathy.  Humor emerges through sharp wit and occasional sex jokes.  She fears missing patterns more than being wrong.  She pretends confidence she doesn’t feel because appearing uncertain makes others panic.\n+* **Arc:** Moves from detached observer to invested partner.  She teaches Jhace to optimize his technique and reduce cost.  She learns that people aren’t structures; human patterns are messier.  She goes from refusing to rest to allowing herself to be cared for.\n+\n+#### Bram Kelter\n+\n+* **Age:** 52 Turns; forgemaster and mentor figure.\n+* **Role:** Grounding force, comic relief, voice of experience.  He regrets compromising his innovations for guild approval.  He sees his younger self in Jhace and doesn’t want him to burn out.  He provides wry commentary and practical support.\n+\n+#### Other Supporting Characters\n+\n+* **Chloen:** Teen apprentice who looks up to Jhace.  Saved in Ch 3 by his first Soulpulse; leg heals inexplicably.\n+* **Pella:** Tiffani’s apprentice.  Brings her food and learns observation.  Mirrors Tiffani’s younger self but with healthier patterns.\n+* **Kylar:** Soulpulse trainee.  Fails catastrophically on first attempt.  Shows that not everyone can handle power, but failure can teach humility.\n+* **Corlexi:** RRO official.  Represents systematic order.  Begins pressing for registration and control of Soulpulse users.  Unknowingly one of four mortal vessels for cosmic forces.\n+* **Salee:** Community organizer.  Believes relationships and networks save lives.  Establishes mutual aid in the Dregs.  Another mortal vessel.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### SOULPULSE MECHANICS IN N01\n+\n+Summarized from `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n+\n+* **Activation:** Requires skin contact with the material and a desperate need to protect.  Casual attempts fail.\n+* **Visual:** Copper‑gold glow spreads from the user’s hands into the material.  Vein‑lines appear on their arms.\n+* **Cost:** The more you use, the worse the tremor and pain.  Stage 1 emerges after 2–3 uses (minor tremor).  By the end of the book Jhace’s tremor never fully fades and faint vein‑lines remain visible; he’s on the cusp of Stage 2.\n+* **Unknowns:** Characters don’t know why Soulpulse exists, what powers it, or the risk of further stages.  They have no language for “Stage 3” or “Stage 4.”\n+\n+---\n+\n+### RELATIONSHIP SUMMARY\n+\n+For full dynamics by chapter, see `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`.  Key arcs:\n+\n+* **Jhace & Tiffani:** From mutual misconceptions to professional respect to deep partnership and romantic acknowledgment.  They end the book committed to working together and being together but are physically too exhausted for sex.\n+* **Jhace & Bram:** Mentorship and camaraderie.  Bram is protective but cannot stop Jhace; he can only slow the burn.\n+* **Tiffani & Pella:** Mentor/student showing healthier pattern recognition habits.\n+* **Jhace & Chloen:** Reluctant hero/hero‑worship, hinting at the next generation.\n+* **J/T & Corlexi:** Brewing political conflict.  They resist registration; he sees them as unpredictable variables.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### CHAPTER STRUCTURE AND INTERLUDES\n+\n+* N01 has **15 chapters**, each mapped to a beat from the `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` (Observation→Recognition, Crisis→Complication, etc.).  The precise mapping is in `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`.\n+* **Interlude I** occurs after Ch 6 and shows Corlexi’s perspective.  It sets up the institutional response and seeds the registration conflict.\n+* **Interlude II** comes after Ch 11 and shows Salee’s perspective.  It introduces communal networks and contrasts with RRO bureaucracy.\n+* The **Epilogue** is a clinical report by the Auditor, a mysterious observer.  It logs data: Jhace’s Stage, partnership formation, positions of the four vessels, and seal integrity percentage (87% at N01 end).  It does not reveal motivations or cosmic truth.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### THEMES AND TONES\n+\n+* **Cost of Power:** Every miracle has a price.  Jhace’s hands shake by the end of the book; Tiffani goes without sleep; Bram regrets his compromises.\n+* **Responsibility vs. Capacity:** You can’t save everyone.  The heroes grapple with guilt and triage decisions.  The story insists on hard choices.\n+* **Partnership as Survival:** Neither Jhace nor Tiffani can succeed alone.  Collaboration is life‑saving.\n+* **Institutional Control vs. Grassroots Response:** The RRO tries to regulate; guilds hoard knowledge; Salee’s networks share resources.  Tensions build.\n+* **Precision as Empathy:** Tiffani’s standards are a form of care.  Bram’s jokes and Tiffani’s innuendo lighten darkness.\n+* **Humor and Humanity:** There is room for jokes, awkward compliments and sex‑tinged banter even in tragedy.  It humanizes characters.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### CONSTRAINTS SUMMARY\n+\n+* **Lexicon:** Use Tide, Anneal, Span, etc.  Never use Earth units.  Avoid modern slang.  See `Constraint_Systems.md` for details.\n+* **Cost:** Show tremor and exhaustion every time Soulpulse is used.  Describe symptoms instead of labels like “Stage 2.”\n+* **Knowledge:** Characters do not know about gods, seals, or cosmic war.  They do not anticipate events in later books.\n+* **Beats:** Follow beat assignments per chapter; avoid repeating structures.  Keep emotional pacing unpredictable.\n+* **Voice:** Each POV has distinct diction and rhythm.  Do not homogenize voices.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### WHAT HAPPENS NEXT (NOT FOR PROSE)\n+\n+This section is for human reference only.  It does not enter the story.\n+\n+* **N02 Preview:** Partnership deepens; sexual relationship begins; political opposition intensifies; Stage 2 is fully established; faction violence starts.\n+* **N03–N05:** Mistakes, codependency warnings, eventual tragedy (Tiffani dies in N05) and Fathom Break (seal shattering) lead to a larger conflict.\n+* **N06–N30:** Gods awaken, divine war ensues, Jhace becomes a weapon, etc.  None of these details should appear in N01.\n+\n+---\n+\n+### CRITICAL REMINDERS\n+\n+* N01 is an origin story.  It’s about discovery, trust and commitment.  It is not about gods or cosmic structures.\n+* The Prose Generation workflow and Validator must be used on every chapter.  They enforce lexicon, cost, voice, knowledge and beat rules.\n+* When in doubt, choose the smaller, human moment.  Don’t rush epic reveals.\n+\n+---\n+\n+**Scope Note:**  This story bible covers everything N01 characters and readers can know.  Later novellas will expand understanding and reveal hidden truths.  For now, respect the boundaries and hold the line on mystery.\n*** End Patch\nEOF\n"]}
